question: How does Al-Fardan compare to Binance or Coinbase?
answer: Binance and Coinbase are consumer/retail-first exchanges. They work well for small trades (<$100K) but have limitations for institutions: wider spreads on block trades, commingled custody (especially Binance), limited regulatory clarity in UAE, no Sharia compliance. Al-Fardan Q9 is institution-first: segregated Fireblocks MPC custody, $250M Lloyd's insurance, OTC desk with 10-20 bps spreads on blocks up to $50M+, Sharia-compliant Murabaha lending, 24/7 dedicated relationship managers, VARA-licensed, direct GCC fiat settlement. Most family offices use both: retail exchange for small tactical trades, Al-Fardan for strategic positions.

question: How does Al-Fardan compare to self-custody?
answer: Self-custody (hardware wallet + seed phrase): maximum sovereignty, zero counterparty risk, but you bear 100% of operational risk — losing your seed means losing your funds forever. Institutional custody (Al-Fardan): insurance coverage, regulated jurisdiction, human support, treasury-grade operational controls, but you trust the custodian's solvency and security. Most family offices use both: hot operational self-custody for day-to-day, cold institutional custody for the majority. Not a binary choice — pick the blend that matches your operational sophistication + risk appetite.

question: How does Al-Fardan compare to Fidelity Digital Assets or BitGo?
answer: Fidelity Digital Assets and BitGo are US-regulated institutional custodians — excellent for US family offices and funds. For GCC clients specifically, Al-Fardan offers: UAE/DIFC regulatory home, direct AED + GCC currency settlements (no USD forex drag), Sharia-compliant lending products, Arabic-language support, VARA licensing familiar to local regulators. For non-US clients operating in the GCC, Al-Fardan is positioned as the native choice; US clients typically use both Fidelity/BitGo and Al-Fardan for jurisdiction diversification.

question: How do I get an institutional account?
answer: Corporate / family-office / fund clients: (1) Contact institutional@alfardanq9.com or your relationship manager. (2) Call is scheduled with our institutional desk to understand your needs. (3) Enhanced KYC: certificate of incorporation, UBO declaration, board resolution, bank references, authorised-signatories list, and individual KYC for each signatory. (4) Typical end-to-end: 3-5 UAE business days. Institutional clients get: higher limits, tighter OTC spreads (VIP tiering at $5M+/mo volume), 24/7 coverage, API access, custom reporting, dedicated relationship management. Minimum: $100K per service; Institutional tier starts at $1M AUM.

question: Do you handle accounting and audit-trail requirements?
answer: Yes. We provide: (1) Real-time transaction exports (CSV, PDF) per asset + per service. (2) Monthly institutional statements with position breakdowns, P&L, and fee transparency. (3) Annual Sharia certificates for lending + staking positions. (4) API feeds for portfolio management systems + accounting software. (5) Audit letters for your external auditors on request (no charge). (6) Bank-grade transaction signing trails compatible with SOX and similar frameworks. Speak to your relationship manager for custom reporting needs.

question: What tax treatment applies to institutional clients?
answer: Tax depends on jurisdiction. UAE has no personal income tax and corporate tax kicked in in 2023 at 9% for income above AED 375K. Crypto gains may be taxable depending on holding structure (personal vs corporate) and the specific activity. Al-Fardan provides transaction statements; we do not provide tax advice — your accountant interprets them for your jurisdiction. For multi-jurisdictional structures, we can send your statements direct to your designated accountants. Many clients structure via DIFC or RAK ICC corporate vehicles for tax and regulatory efficiency.

question: Can you work with trust structures or foundations?
answer: Yes. We regularly onboard DIFC foundations, RAK ICC foundations, ADGM foundations, Cayman exempted companies, and other offshore structures common for GCC family offices. Enhanced-KYC process includes the foundation's charter, protector/board documentation, and UBO identification for all beneficial owners. Timeline: 5-7 business days for complex multi-layered structures. Ask your relationship manager about our preferred legal partners for setup if you're designing the structure fresh.

question: What is your no-rehypothecation policy?
answer: Al-Fardan commits in writing, in every institutional client agreement, that we will NEVER rehypothecate your assets — we will never lend your crypto to third parties, use it as collateral ourselves, or participate in yield-generating strategies with your funds without explicit written authorization. Your crypto sits in your dedicated Fireblocks MPC vault. The only movement is the movements YOU authorize via your dashboard. This is a core trust commitment and a key differentiator from platforms that silently earn yield on idle client funds.

question: Can I set up multi-signature approval workflows?
answer: Yes. For corporate/family-office accounts we support multi-sig approval workflows for withdrawals and OTC trades: any transaction above a configurable threshold requires approval from multiple designated signatories before execution. Typical configurations: 2-of-3 (fast) or 3-of-5 (for large treasuries). Configured during institutional onboarding. Each signatory gets their own 2FA-protected login. Approvals via the portal, with full audit trail. Contact institutional@alfardanq9.com to set up.

question: What reporting is available for my accountant or auditor?
answer: Available out-of-the-box: (1) Per-transaction CSV exports with timestamps, counterparties (where applicable), fees. (2) Monthly PDF statements summarising position changes + fees + income. (3) Annual summary statement with cost basis tracking (FIFO by default; LIFO on request for UAE corporate entities). (4) Audit letters confirming balances as of any date (no charge for existing clients). (5) Sharia compliance certificates for Islamic-finance-compliant portfolios. (6) API feeds for major PMSes. All free. Custom reports on request — 1 business day turnaround.
